text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in the foresty grove of Elderglen, a wise old owl named Orion heard a distant moan.

Orion hooted mournfully and flew off on a secret mission. On his adventure, he met a wise old owl named Willow.

Willow listened intently while Orion recounted his plight. An old enemy had stolen his favorite mate's last feather.

Willow pondered deeply. After a great delay, he hooted triumphantly!

He swooped down into the tree hollow where the feather lay. There sat a clumsy forest creature clutching the quill pad of an invisible crayon.

Willow retrieved the feather. He gave it to Orion.

The old owl gobbled down his mate's feather piecing by piecing.

Orion thanked Willow endlessly. They hooted a friendly farewell before becoming invisible under the moonlight.

With feather restoration complete, Orion returned home.

Heeroooby-dooby-dooooo!
